title: Error on HyperV VM start when gvproxy has failed to start
body:
After the VM has successfully started, check that gvproxy is still running. If it is not, throw an error and refuse to complete machine start.

[CI:DOCS] I don't think we can deliberately trigger a bad gvproxy start without a bad Podman binary. We could try and kill gvproxy after it starts but before the machine is booted but that's very prone to races.

Test failures continue after rerun. Mostly different tests, a few of the usual suspects failed twice.

@mheon neither pass locally for me either ... and one thing i am seeing is that each test is leaving behind a spurious gvproxy process.  i get the same failure with the main branch .. we probably need to chase that down now?
Is this just during the tests? I didn't see this when I was verifying, but I did everything manually.
Rebased on latest main. Behaves identically to main in local test runs.
